resources.string: You can change your name and username at any time
Select everything that resonates with you
How do you prefer to express yourself?
You can always change your mind
I prefer putting thoughts on paper
I\'m more comfortable talking it out
Before we begin, we want to be transparent about how we handle your data.
We collect mood, emotion, and journaling data to provide personalized insights and improve your experience.
Your diary entries are analyzed locally and via AI to offer reflections and suggestions. They are never shared with third parties.
If you use voice chat, audio is processed in real-time for the AI conversation and is not stored on our servers.
I have read and consent to the collection and processing of my wellness data as described above.
You can revoke consent and delete your data at any time from Settings.
You\'ll need to complete the setup to use the app.
Unlock the full potential of your journey
Everything unlimited: diaries, AI chat, avatar, insights, social
We\'re preparing Calmify PRO with exclusive features. Sign up for priority access and a special launch discount.
We\'ll notify you as soon as PRO is available. Thanks for believing in Calmify!

Calmify is NOT a medical device and does NOT provide diagnosis, treatment, or medical advice. Wellness features (mood tracking, journaling, AI chat) are self-reflection tools and DO NOT replace professional healthcare advice. If you are experiencing a crisis, contact your doctor or emergency services.
All your data will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
Deleting your account will permanently remove all your data. This action cannot be undone.
Thank you for taking time for yourself. Your answers have been saved and will be analyzed to offer you personalized wellness insights.
Start small. One habit at a time.
e.g. Sit down and close your eyes
Your diaries and conversations will appear here
With Calmify Pro you get unlimited conversations with Eve, advanced insights and much more.
How can I help you today?
Start a conversation to experience natural voice
With Calmify Pro you get live sessions up to 15 minutes with Eve and much more.
Allow camera access to enhance Gemini\'s visual understanding
Customize how Eve interacts with you
Test animations, visemes and idle loop
Help improve mental health research with anonymized data
Enable detailed psychological analysis and recommendations
Your personal information helps us provide tailored insights and recommendations.
Too little sleep can impact mental health
Try to aim for 7-9 hours for optimal well-being
Great! This is in the recommended range
Excessive sleep can also affect mood
Update your daily habits and routines
Lifestyle factors like sleep, exercise and social support influence your wellbeing.
How would you describe your social support network?
Your mental health information is confidential and used only to personalize your experience.
Have you been diagnosed with any mental health conditions?
This app is not a substitute for professional medical advice.
Update your goals and coping strategies
Setting clear goals and identifying effective coping strategies helps you make progress.
What do you want to achieve?
How do you like to cope?
Select coping strategies that work for you
Great! We\'ll use this to personalize your experience.
How do you want Eve to talk to you?
How often do you want to receive a writing reminder?
Eve won\'t bring up these topics unless you do first.
An error occurred in the UI
Would you like to share how you feel with the community?
What is blocking you right now?
What\'s the smallest thing you can do RIGHT NOW, even imperfectly?
There\'s too much in your head. Let\'s do a brain dump: write down EVERYTHING running through your mind.
That\'s a story, not a fact. Let\'s reframe this thought together.
When the mind gets stuck, move the body. Take a 20-minute walk and we\'ll talk again.
Tell me what\'s happening. You don\'t have to analyze — just narrate.
No need to analyze, just narrate. Write freely what you feel.
You have %1$d active blocks. You\'ve got this!
💡 Here\'s what you can do now
Pick the tool that feels most fitting right now:
Write down everything on your mind
Calm the mind with a guided breath
Your intention for the next month
Four lights, one meeting point. Light the first when you feel ready.
A detail, a color, a gesture, a sound...
What area does it relate to?
The words will come on their own...
How do they lower their emotional barriers?
e.g. Teasing, silent presence, concrete gestures...
e.g. Being ignored, feeling useless, silence...
How do they protect themselves emotionally?
e.g. Sarcasm, detachment, changing the subject...
How often do they want to interact?
What do they hope to get from conversations?
e.g. Better understanding themselves, having someone listen...
What do they NEVER want to be told?
e.g. Don\'t judge my choices, don\'t talk about...
The wound that never fully closes
What is absolutely non-negotiable for them?
The wound that never fully closes...
The thing they do better than anyone else...
e.g. Grew up in a Southern Italian family...
e.g. Spike Spiegel, Amelie Poulain, Naruto...
This is your home. Every day you find a personalized greeting and a summary of your journey.
From here you can access with one tap: diary, meditation, chat with Eve and much more.
Write how you feel, analyze your thoughts and build awareness over time.
Eve is your AI companion. She listens, reflects and helps you explore your emotions.
Track how you feel every day to discover patterns and trends in your wellbeing.
Deep analysis based on your diaries and conversations. Discover what truly moves you.
Build positive routines, one small step at a time.
See how you\'ve grown over time. Data, charts and reflections on your journey.
Connect with other inner explorers. Share what you want, hide the rest.
Too many attempts, try again later
Are you sure you want to sign out?
Failed to sign out. Please try again.
This action cannot be undone. All your diaries will be permanently deleted.
Type something in the bar above
Your avatar has been created successfully.
How do they react under pressure?
Take 2 minutes to check in on yourself
Answers are visible only to you and used to generate your insights.
This is your home. Every day you\'ll find a personalized greeting and a summary of how you\'re doing.
From here you can access your journal, meditation, energy check-in and more with one tap. Choose what you need today.
Your daily mood is shown here. Tap it to update or to see how it has changed over time.
Your avatar is always ready to listen. Tap it to start a voice or text conversation, anytime.
Account deleted. All data has been removed.
This section will be available soon.
The trend is improving compared to previous weeks.
The trend shows a decline — no need to panic, it\'s useful information.
Your path is consistent, and consistency has value.
Write more to get a complete picture.
Weekend morning, no rush. How do you feel today?
The afternoon is a good moment to pause. What do you notice within yourself?
The calm of yesterday — can you find it again tonight?
This is your safe space. How are you, really?
You\'ve already written today. How has the day evolved?
A second thought for today? Sometimes things shift in the afternoon.
Before sleeping: what are you taking with you from this day?
Welcome back. No matter how much time has passed — you\'re here now.
%1$d days in a row. You\'re building something important.
%1$d days in a row — consistency makes the difference. How is today?
A new day. With what intention do you want to live it?
Mid-morning. Has something caught your attention?
How are you spending this free time?
The day is closing. What was the truest moment?
Before letting this day go — what do you want to remember?
Last time wasn\'t easy. How are you today?
You were feeling lonely. Has something changed?
There was tension in the air. Has it eased a little?
Yesterday\'s anger — is it still there or has it transformed?
Yesterday there was disappointment. Can you see it differently today?
You wrote something difficult. How is it going now?
You were in good spirits yesterday. Can you bring that energy into today?
This week you\'ve shown a positive and stable mood.
A balanced week, with ups and downs.
It hasn\'t been an easy week — and that\'s okay.
Stress has been high. Take care of yourself.
Stress has been there but you\'ve handled it.
Stress levels have stayed low — great signal.
Your resilience is solid — you bounce back well after difficulties.
%1$d journals this week: you\'re building an important habit.
It indicates how many consecutive days you have written in your journal. Building a continuous streak reinforces the habit and helps you stay consistent in your growth journey.
A short title for your journal
Write how you feel, what happened
How intense is the emotion you feel?
How much energy do you have?
How calm or anxious do you feel?
What do you feel in your body?
Add photos and save your journal
Write everything on your mind.\nWorries, ideas, tasks, emotions.\nIt doesn\'t have to make sense.
What are you grateful for today?
When did you go to bed?
What beauty did you notice today?
Define your personal practice in Settings and add it as a habit in the Habit Tracker.
Write the thought that blocks you, exactly as you feel it.
Put the thought under the lens.
Not toxic positivity. An honest, credible alternative.
e.g. \"I\'m not capable of doing anything well\"
What evidence do you have FOR?
Are there moments when it wasn\'t true?
How would a friend who loves you say it?
In a year, how much will this thought weigh?
Now rewrite it honestly and credibly:
e.g. \"I\'m learning and have already made progress in...\"
What did you do for someone?
Who did you spend time with?
Who are you grateful to today?
How did you spend your time?
Writing your thoughts helps you process emotions and find clarity. The journal is the heart of your growth path.
Free your mind from everything that occupies it, without judgment and without structure.
Recognizing daily beauty trains the brain to positivity.
Breath and presence to calm the mind. Just a few minutes a day make a difference.
Transform limiting thoughts with the CBT technique. Examine, challenge, rewrite.
Identify what is stopping you and find a way around it. Awareness is the first step.
Observe thoughts that recur to you. Recognizing them frees you from automatism.
Track how your body feels every day. Energy, sleep, hydration, movement.
Track your sleep to understand quality and duration. Good sleep is essential.
Record physical activity, even if minimal. Every movement counts.
Overview of your physical wellbeing. Trends, averages, observations.
Discover what truly matters to you. Your personal values guide your choices.
Find the intersection between passion, talent, mission and profession. Your reason for living.
Cultivate the wonder of small things. The world is full of beauty waiting to be noticed.
Practice the void to make space. In silence the deepest answers arise.
Cultivate the relationships that matter. Connection is essential nourishment.
Collect what inspires you: quotes, beauty, ideas. Build your library of meaning.
Build small daily habits. Consistency wins over intensity.
Design the context to make good choices easier. Environment shapes behavior.
How satisfied are you with your life overall?
How satisfied are you with your work or studies?
How would you rate the quality of your relationships?
How present and mindful are you in the moment?
How much do you feel you have a purpose or direction in life?
How grateful do you feel for what you have?
How much do you feel in control of your choices and life?
How capable and competent do you feel in what you do?
How connected and close do you feel to important people in your life?
How lonely do you feel in this period?

Cognitive patterns are recurring schemas in the way you think and interpret situations. For example, always tending to catastrophize or seeing things only in black and white. Recognizing them doesn\'t mean you\'re \"broken\" — it\'s simply the first step to choosing different responses.

Sleeping well and feeling well are closely connected: fewer hours of sleep often translate into lower mood the next day. Calmify cross-references your sleep and mood notes over time to show this specific relationship in your life.

A psychological model identifying three fundamental needs for wellbeing: autonomy (feeling free to choose), competence (feeling capable), and connection (feeling close to others). When these three needs are met, motivation is authentic and lasting.

The trend shows how your overall wellbeing has changed over time, combining mood, energy, sleep and quality of reflections. Don\'t seek perfection: even a small constant improvement over time has a huge impact.

Measures how strongly you feel an emotion right now, regardless of which one. An intense emotion is neither good nor bad: it just shows how much it\'s involving you. Noticing intensity helps you understand which situations affect you most.
Stress is a natural body response to perceived challenges. Tracking your stress level over time helps identify the moments or contexts that weigh most on you, so you can act in a targeted way.

This scale captures your inner alert state right now. Anxiety is not a flaw: it\'s the brain trying to protect you. Logging it regularly lets you see recurring triggers and, over time, respond with more awareness.

Slow, mindful breathing activates the parasympathetic nervous system — the one that \"brakes\" the stress response. A few minutes are enough to feel a real difference in muscle tone and mental clarity.

A technique that links a new habit to one already established. Example: \"After breakfast, I write three things I\'m grateful for\". Using an existing behaviour as an \"anchor\" makes it much easier to build something new.

The smallest possible entry point for a habit. Instead of \"meditating for 30 minutes\", the minimum action is \"sit on the cushion\". Lowering the initial threshold makes it almost impossible not to start — and you often end up doing much more.
Pick how long you have, what you need, and we\'ll choose a breath technique to match. Always second person, always your pace.
So we offer only what\'s right for your body today.
Calming circle, gentle counts, your nose-led breath.
A short reflection at the end
What you may notice, and what builds with practice.
This is a wellness tool, not medical treatment. If you feel dizzy, lightheaded, tingling, nauseous, or short of breath at any point, stop and breathe naturally.
Tick anything that applies. We\'ll keep you on a gentle track if any do — natural-breath only, no pacing or holds.
if you feel dizzy, lightheaded, tingling, nauseous, or short of breath. The Stop button is always reachable.
None of these apply to me right now.
You can re-screen any time before a session.
Based on what you ticked, we\'ll guide you through natural-breath observation today — no pacing override, no holds. Talk to a clinician about pacing techniques in person.

Calmify is a wellness tool, not a medical service. This screening helps us avoid breathing techniques that could be unsafe in certain conditions, but it isn\'t a substitute for advice from your clinician.
uncontrolled hypertension, recent cardiac event, arrhythmia, heart failure
Severe asthma, COPD, or active respiratory infection
Recent abdominal, chest, or eye surgery
Currently driving, operating machinery, or in/near water
Pick a length you can give without watching the clock.
We\'ll match a technique to your goal — you can change it below.
How familiar with breathwork are you?
Welcome. We\'ll start you on coherent breathing — equal, gentle breaths, easiest on the body.
We picked this for you. Tap to swap if you prefer another.
Locked to the gentle track today.
Wellness tool, not medical care. Stop and breathe naturally if anything feels off.
Equal, gentle breaths around 5.5 per minute — nervous-system reset.
At about 5.5 breaths a minute, heart rate variability rises and the vagus nerve signals safety, easing the body out of stress mode.
Through the nose. Belly soft, shoulders quiet.
Let the breath find a smooth, even rhythm.
If your body wants more air, take more air.
Longer exhales than inhales — the most reliable way to downshift.
A longer exhale activates the parasympathetic (\"rest and digest\") branch of your nervous system, slowing heart rate and softening tension.
Inhale through the nose. Exhale slowly through the nose or softly through pursed lips.
Let the exhale be unhurried — never forced.
Belly rises on inhale; releases on exhale.
Four-count square — steady, focused, used by clinicians and pilots.
Equal, contained intervals create a metronome the mind can follow, narrowing attention and calming reactivity.
Holds are gentle pauses, not breath-holding contests.
If a hold feels too long, shorten it — the rhythm is yours.
Through the nose, soft and even.
Dr. Weil\'s pattern. Strong parasympathetic effect — capped at 4 cycles.
A long hold and longer exhale amplify the vagal response. Even a few cycles can noticeably slow your pulse.
Exhale slowly through the mouth, lips slightly pursed.
Four cycles is plenty. If you feel lightheaded, return to natural breath.
Soft, deep breath into the belly. No pacing — just steady awareness.
Engaging the diaphragm instead of the chest deepens oxygen exchange and signals safety to the body.
One hand on your belly, one on your chest.
On the inhale, only the belly hand should rise.
No counting. Let the rhythm be yours.
Sweep attention through the body. Breath stays exactly as it is.
Naming sensation without changing it interrupts rumination and grounds you in the present moment.
Notice your feet. Your legs. Your hips.
Find a comfortable seat. Relax your shoulders, soften your jaw.
Let your eyes rest, or close them. Notice your natural breath, without changing it.
No pacing yet. Just arriving, here.
Let the pacing go. Breathe how your body wants to.
Notice what\'s shifted — a softer chest, a slower thought, anything at all.
When you\'re ready, slowly open your eyes. Return to the room.
You can stop any time. We\'ll show a short reflection on what you completed so far.
A slower pulse, or a softer chest and shoulders.
A small gap between thought and reaction.
A clearer signal when your body is ready to rest.

People who practice daily often report calmer baseline mood, steadier sleep, and a lower stress response in everyday situations. Frequency matters more than duration — a few minutes daily beats a long session weekly.
Pair it with a habit you already have.
Three breaths after you sit down at your desk, or before you check your phone.
Two minutes of %1$s before a hard conversation can change how it lands.
This is a wellness tool, not medical treatment. Persistent anxiety, panic, sleep issues, or breathing difficulty warrant a conversation with a clinician.
What we have, where from, and what\'s been sent.
Health Connect isn\'t supported on this device
No data yet — connect a device to start
We never send raw data points to our servers. Only aggregated statistics.
Every sample, aggregate, and consent record will be removed from this device and our servers. This can\'t be undone. Health Connect itself is not affected — your data stays with your wearable.
Some data types failed — see logs
We don\'t sell your data. We don\'t use it for advertising. We don\'t share it with third parties without your explicit consent.
Exactly what we have, where each piece came from, and what we\'ve sent to the server. No softer words.
Raw samples stay on this device.
Only weekly and monthly aggregates leave your phone — never individual readings, never your location.
Calmify can read steps, sleep and heart-rate context from your phone or wearable.
Counts of the actual readings sitting in Calmify\'s on-device store. Nothing here is in the cloud.
We have %1$d aggregates pending sync. Last successful sync: %2$s. 0 raw samples have ever been transmitted.
Aggregates only. Never raw, never per-second, never location.
How sure we are about each signal
Confidence reflects sampling density and source consistency — it isn\'t a quality judgment of your device.
No connected source has contributed enough data yet to estimate confidence.
Take everything with you. Or take it back.
GDPR rights are one tap away, not buried six menus deep.

Export returns a single JSON file with every local sample and every aggregate we\'ve sent. Delete removes the local store and asks the server to erase the aggregates linked to your account — usually within 30 days.
These aren\'t aspirations — they\'re commitments we\'d be liable for breaking.
Calmify does not sell your data.
Not to anyone, not in any form.
Calmify does not use your data for advertising.
There are no ads in the app and there never will be.
Calmify does not share data with third parties
without an explicit, per-purpose opt-in from you.
Aggregates train the wellbeing model anonymously.
You can opt out without losing the app\'s features.
Read the full Privacy Policy · Data processing agreement · last reviewed May 2026.
Delete everything we hold about your body?

This removes every local sample and asks the server to erase the aggregates linked to your account. The action is irreversible — Calmify has no backup of biological data outside what you\'d be deleting.
Type exactly \"%1$s\" to enable the delete button.

Every Sunday night and the first of each month, Calmify computes statistics from the raw samples on this device — means, variability, totals — and sends only those numbers. Individual readings, timestamps below the day level, and any location data are never transmitted.

The server uses these aggregates to keep your wellbeing model warm across reinstalls, and (anonymously, if you\'ve opted in) to improve the model for everyone. You can opt out at any time without losing any feature.
Calmify is a wellness tool, not a medical service. Biological data is used as context for your wellbeing — never as a score or target.
Want your body to talk to you?
Sometimes the body knows before the mind does. With your permission, Calmify can quietly listen to your wearable — not to score you, but to help you notice patterns you might miss.
Nothing changes about how Calmify works without this. You can turn it on, off, or piece-by-piece, anytime, from Settings.
Each signal is its own switch. Tap the icon to see what it actually is.
Beats per minute. Reflects how aroused or calm you are right now.
Variability between beats. Often the earliest signal of stress — sometimes before you feel it.
Stages, duration, awakenings. Sleep is the foundation everything else rests on.
Daily step totals. Not a goal, not a streak — just one input among many.
Your nightly average. A slow drift over weeks is often the most honest signal.
Blood oxygen during sleep. Helpful when your breathing isn\'t obvious from anything else.
Workouts your watch picks up automatically. We never set targets.
Bio is context, not a score.
Journal that notices when you didn\'t sleep
A bad night nudges tomorrow\'s prompt toward gentleness instead of optimization.
Meditation that confirms what you felt
After a session, a small note: your heart rate slowed by 8 bpm. The body kept score.
Insight that connects dots you missed
Weeks where you walked more had calmer evenings. Patterns, gently surfaced.
Calmify reads from your phone\'s health hub — Health Connect on Android, HealthKit on iOS. We never write to it, and we never share what we read.
Health Connect isn\'t installed. Tap below to get it from the Play Store.
Over the next few days, we\'ll begin to notice the small ways your body shows up in how you feel. No scores, no targets — just gentle context.
That\'s a fine place to start.
Calmify works beautifully without any of this. You can turn on bio signals from Settings whenever — or never — you like.
Granular by default — turn just what you want.
Calmify reads these from your phone\'s health hub — never directly from the cloud, never written back. Read our data principles.
We\'ll never hand you a number to chase. Instead, your body\'s signals quietly add depth to what Calmify already does.
WEEKLY PATTERN · LOW CONFIDENCE — keep listening
Patterns are suggestions, not verdicts. You always tell us if they match how you feel.
We never write to your health hub.
Nothing Calmify does shows up in your other health apps.
Signals stay on your device by default.
Only your private journal text and reflections sync with your account.
Settings → Bio signals. We forget within 24 hours.
Not detected on this device. Free, from Google.
Permission granted. We\'ll start gently and learn your patterns over the next few days.
Calmify is a wellness tool, not medical care. Bio signals add context to your reflections — they never replace a clinician.
Granular as you set them. Change any of these anytime.
No signals are being read. Nothing has changed about your experience.
Settings → Bio signals. Every switch from this flow lives there, plus the option to forget everything we\'ve ever read.
A small footer will appear under any insight that uses bio data — "From {device} · medium confidence" — so you always know where a number came from.
What we have, where from, and what\'s been sent
Pair your device via Health Connect — read-only
You slept %1$s. Heart rate this morning is %2$s.
Last night was short — only %1$s. Be gentle with yourself today.
You rested well last night — %1$s. Reflection might come easily today.
Heart rate eased from %1$d down to %2$d bpm. The drop mirrors the breath pacing — the meditation seems to have landed in the body, not just the mind.
Heart rate stayed around %1$d bpm throughout. The body held its rhythm — a gentle, even session.
Heart rate during this session: from %1$d down to %2$d bpm, low %3$d, high %4$d.
HRV improved during the session — see the detail with PRO
Weeks where you meditated %1$d or more times show an average HRV roughly %2$d%% higher than weeks with fewer sessions. Coincidence? Maybe — but the link has held for %3$d weeks running.
Meditation sessions per week and average HRV per week, over the last %1$d weeks
based on the last %1$d days · refreshed today

Your HRV averaged about %1$d ms this week — roughly %2$d%% above your typical %3$d ms. It often shows up after a stretch of better sleep or lighter days. The pattern is not a verdict — just an observation worth holding next to whatever else you noticed this week.

Your HRV averaged about %1$d ms this week — roughly %2$d%% below your typical %3$d ms. Bodies dip for many reasons (stress, travel, a virus brewing, less sleep). The pattern is not a diagnosis — if you\'re noticing the dip elsewhere, it might be a useful nudge to be a little gentler.
Your HRV held close to your usual this week — about %1$d ms against your typical %3$d ms. Steady weeks are easy to overlook, but they\'re often the foundation everything else rests on.
Body data threads quietly into your day
Bio-signal is off. Nothing is being read or stored. Your existing data is preserved until you choose to delete it.
Each signal is opt-in. Turning one off stops new readings immediately — and you can delete what\'s already there.
Resting + during meditation. Powers the body-followed narrative.
Subtle nervous-system signal. Powers PRO correlation insight.
Duration and stages. Threads into the morning narrative.
Light activity context. Never shown as a goal.
Trend baseline. Helps distinguish "off day" from "actually unusual".
Optional. Off by default — read only when you explicitly request it.
Workouts and movement bouts. Context only — never a target.
Devices and apps Calmify is reading from. Each one keeps its own permissions in your OS health hub.
Your body data is yours. Look at what we have, take it elsewhere, or remove it entirely — without asking permission.
What we have, where it came from
Per-signal log of every reading and its source
Permanent · GDPR Art. 17 · cannot be undone
Required only for advanced PRO insights

By default Calmify computes narratives on your phone and only stores small daily summaries. Turning this on sends raw HRV and heart-rate samples (encrypted) to our servers so multi-week correlations can run server-side. You can turn this off and wipe the cloud copy at any time from "Delete all bio data". On-device narratives keep working either way.
Each surface is on by default — and each can be quieted without touching the others.
A single observational line above the composer.
Mini HR chart with the session\'s pacing.
The morning narrative with mini-chips below.
PRO-only weekly correlations between bio and practice.
Viewing, sovereignty and full GDPR are included in Free — and always will be. Pro is for AI insight and multi-week correlations, not for owning your data.
HRV detail in the post-meditation card
Changes save automatically. Calmify never reads body data outside the signals you\'ve turned on above.
Slow shifts often mean more than any single day. Worth noticing, not chasing.
Once your wearable sends a few more samples, the chart will fill in here.
%1$s timeline. Range %2$s to %3$s. Vertical markers show journal entries and meditation sessions.
